root_text.sn51.20:1.1: “Cattārome, bhikkhave, iddhipādā bhāvitā bahulīkatā mahapphalā honti mahānisaṁsā”.
root_text.sn51.20:2.1: Kathaṁ bhāvitā ca, bhikkhave, cattāro iddhipādā kathaṁ bahulīkatā mahapphalā honti mahānisaṁsā?
root_text.sn51.20:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu chandasamādhippadhānasaṅkhārasamannāgataṁ iddhipādaṁ bhāveti—
root_text.sn51.20:2.3: iti me chando na ca atilīno bhavissati, na ca atippaggahito bhavissati, na ca ajjhattaṁ saṅkhitto bhavissati, na ca bahiddhā vikkhitto bhavissati.
root_text.sn51.20:2.5: yathā pure tathā pacchā, yathā pacchā tathā pure;
root_text.sn51.20:2.6: yathā adho tathā uddhaṁ, yathā uddhaṁ tathā adho;
root_text.sn51.20:2.7: yathā divā tathā rattiṁ yathā rattiṁ tathā divā.
root_text.sn51.20:2.8: Iti vivaṭena cetasā apariyonaddhena sappabhāsaṁ cittaṁ bhāveti.
root_text.sn51.20:2.12: iti me vīmaṁsā na ca atilīnā bhavissati, na ca atippaggahitā bhavissati, na ca ajjhattaṁ saṅkhittā bhavissati, na ca bahiddhā vikkhittā bhavissati.
root_text.sn51.20:2.14: yathā pure tathā pacchā, yathā pacchā tathā pure;
root_text.sn51.20:2.15: yathā adho tathā uddhaṁ, yathā uddhaṁ tathā adho;
root_text.sn51.20:2.16: yathā divā tathā rattiṁ, yathā rattiṁ tathā divā.
root_text.sn51.20:2.17: Iti vivaṭena cetasā apariyonaddhena sappabhāsaṁ cittaṁ bhāveti.
root_text.sn51.20:5.1: Katamo ca, bhikkhave, ajjhattaṁ saṅkhitto chando?
root_text.sn51.20:5.3: ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, ajjhattaṁ saṅkhitto chando.
root_text.sn51.20:6.1: Katamo ca, bhikkhave, bahiddhā vikkhitto chando?
root_text.sn51.20:6.2: Yo, bhikkhave, chando bahiddhā pañca kāmaguṇe ārabbha anuvikkhitto anuvisaṭo—
root_text.sn51.20:6.3: ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, bahiddhā vikkhitto chando.
root_text.sn51.20:7.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu pacchāpuresaññī ca viharati—
root_text.sn51.20:7.2: yathā pure tathā pacchā, yathā pacchā tathā pure?
root_text.sn51.20:7.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno pacchāpuresaññā suggahitā hoti sumanasikatā sūpadhāritā suppaṭividdhā paññāya.
root_text.sn51.20:7.4: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu pacchāpuresaññī ca viharati—
root_text.sn51.20:7.5: yathā pure tathā pacchā, yathā pacchā tathā pure.
root_text.sn51.20:8.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yathā adho tathā uddhaṁ, yathā uddhaṁ tathā adho viharati?
root_text.sn51.20:8.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu imameva kāyaṁ uddhaṁ pādatalā adho kesamatthakā tacapariyantaṁ pūraṁ nānappakārassa asucino paccavekkhati:
root_text.sn51.20:8.3: ‘atthi imasmiṁ kāye kesā lomā nakhā dantā taco maṁsaṁ nhāru aṭṭhi aṭṭhimiñjaṁ vakkaṁ hadayaṁ yakanaṁ kilomakaṁ pihakaṁ papphāsaṁ antaṁ antaguṇaṁ udariyaṁ karīsaṁ pittaṁ semhaṁ pubbo lohitaṁ sedo medo assu vasā kheḷo siṅghāṇikā lasikā muttan’ti.
root_text.sn51.20:8.4: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yathā adho tathā uddhaṁ, yathā uddhaṁ tathā adho viharati.
root_text.sn51.20:9.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yathā divā tathā rattiṁ, yathā rattiṁ tathā divā viharati?
root_text.sn51.20:9.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yehi ākārehi yehi liṅgehi yehi nimittehi divā chandasamādhippadhānasaṅkhārasamannāgataṁ iddhipādaṁ bhāveti, so tehi ākārehi tehi liṅgehi tehi nimittehi rattiṁ chandasamādhippadhānasaṅkhārasamannāgataṁ iddhipādaṁ bhāveti;
root_text.sn51.20:9.3: yehi vā pana ākārehi yehi liṅgehi yehi nimittehi rattiṁ chandasamādhippadhānasaṅkhārasamannāgataṁ iddhipādaṁ bhāveti, so tehi ākārehi tehi liṅgehi tehi nimittehi divā chandasamādhippadhānasaṅkhārasamannāgataṁ iddhipādaṁ bhāveti.
root_text.sn51.20:9.4: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yathā divā tathā rattiṁ, yathā rattiṁ tathā divā viharati.
root_text.sn51.20:10.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu vivaṭena cetasā apariyonaddhena sappabhāsaṁ cittaṁ bhāveti?
root_text.sn51.20:10.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno ālokasaññā suggahitā hoti divāsaññā svādhiṭṭhitā.
root_text.sn51.20:10.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu vivaṭena cetasā apariyonaddhena sappabhāsaṁ cittaṁ bhāveti.
root_text.sn51.20:13.3: idaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, ajjhattaṁ saṅkhittaṁ vīriyaṁ.
root_text.sn51.20:14.2: Yaṁ, bhikkhave, vīriyaṁ bahiddhā pañca kāmaguṇe ārabbha anuvikkhittaṁ anuvisaṭaṁ—idaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, bahiddhā vikkhittaṁ vīriyaṁ …pe….
root_text.sn51.20:15.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu vivaṭena cetasā apariyonaddhena sappabhāsaṁ cittaṁ bhāveti?
root_text.sn51.20:15.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno ālokasaññā suggahitā hoti divāsaññā svādhiṭṭhitā.
root_text.sn51.20:15.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu vivaṭena cetasā apariyonaddhena sappabhāsaṁ cittaṁ bhāveti.
root_text.sn51.20:18.3: idaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, ajjhattaṁ saṅkhittaṁ cittaṁ.
root_text.sn51.20:19.2: Yaṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ bahiddhā pañca kāmaguṇe ārabbha anuvikkhittaṁ anuvisaṭaṁ—
root_text.sn51.20:19.3: idaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, bahiddhā vikkhittaṁ cittaṁ …pe…
root_text.sn51.20:19.4: evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu vivaṭena cetasā apariyonaddhena sappabhāsaṁ cittaṁ bhāveti.
root_text.sn51.20:22.1: Katamā ca, bhikkhave, ajjhattaṁ saṅkhittā vīmaṁsā?
root_text.sn51.20:22.3: ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, ajjhattaṁ saṅkhittā vīmaṁsā.
root_text.sn51.20:23.1: Katamā ca, bhikkhave, bahiddhā vikkhittā vīmaṁsā?
root_text.sn51.20:23.2: Yā, bhikkhave, vīmaṁsā bahiddhā pañca kāmaguṇe ārabbha anuvikkhittā anuvisaṭā—
root_text.sn51.20:23.3: ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, bahiddhā vikkhittā vīmaṁsā …pe…
root_text.sn51.20:23.4: evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu vivaṭena cetasā apariyonaddhena sappabhāsaṁ cittaṁ bhāveti.
root_text.sn51.20:23.5: Evaṁ bhāvitā kho, bhikkhave, cattāro iddhipādā evaṁ bahulīkatā mahapphalā honti mahānisaṁsā.
root_text.sn51.20:24.1: Evaṁ bhāvitesu kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu catūsu iddhipādesu evaṁ bahulīkatesu, anekavihitaṁ iddhividhaṁ paccanubhoti—ekopi hutvā bahudhā hoti, bahudhāpi hutvā eko hoti …pe… yāva brahmalokāpi kāyena vasaṁ vatteti.
root_text.sn51.20:24.2: Evaṁ bhāvitesu kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu catūsu iddhipādesu evaṁ bahulīkatesu, āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharatī”ti.

translation_text.sn51.20:1.1: “Mendicants, when the four bases of psychic power are developed and cultivated they’re very fruitful and beneficial.
translation_text.sn51.20:2.2: It’s when a mendicant develops the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to enthusiasm, and active effort.
translation_text.sn51.20:2.3: They think: ‘My enthusiasm won’t be too lax or too tense. And it’ll be neither constricted internally nor scattered externally.’
translation_text.sn51.20:2.4: And they meditate perceiving before and behind:
translation_text.sn51.20:2.5: as before, so behind; as behind, so before;
translation_text.sn51.20:2.6: as below, so above; as above, so below;
translation_text.sn51.20:2.7: as by day, so by night; as by night, so by day.
translation_text.sn51.20:2.8: And so, with an open and unenveloped heart, they develop a mind that’s full of radiance.
translation_text.sn51.20:2.9: They develop the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to energy …
translation_text.sn51.20:2.12: They think: ‘My inquiry won’t be too lax or too tense. And it’ll be neither constricted internally nor scattered externally.’
translation_text.sn51.20:2.13: And they meditate perceiving before and behind:
translation_text.sn51.20:2.14: as before, so behind; as behind, so before;
translation_text.sn51.20:2.15: as below, so above; as above, so below;
translation_text.sn51.20:2.16: as by day, so by night; as by night, so by day.
translation_text.sn51.20:2.17: And so, with an open and unenveloped heart, they develop a mind that’s full of radiance.
translation_text.sn51.20:3.1: And what is enthusiasm that’s too lax?
translation_text.sn51.20:3.2: It’s when enthusiasm is coupled with laziness.
translation_text.sn51.20:4.1: And what is enthusiasm that’s too tense?
translation_text.sn51.20:4.2: It’s when enthusiasm is coupled with restlessness.
translation_text.sn51.20:5.1: And what is enthusiasm that’s constricted internally?
translation_text.sn51.20:5.2: It’s when enthusiasm is coupled with dullness and drowsiness.
translation_text.sn51.20:5.3: This is called enthusiasm constricted internally.
translation_text.sn51.20:6.1: And what is enthusiasm that’s scattered externally?
translation_text.sn51.20:6.2: It’s when enthusiasm is frequently scattered and diffused externally on account of the five kinds of sensual stimulation.
translation_text.sn51.20:6.3: This is called enthusiasm scattered externally.
translation_text.sn51.20:7.1: And how does a mendicant meditate perceiving before and behind:
translation_text.sn51.20:7.2: as before, so behind; as behind, so before?
translation_text.sn51.20:7.3: It’s when the perception of before and behind is properly grasped, focused on, borne in mind, and penetrated with wisdom by a mendicant.
translation_text.sn51.20:7.4: That’s how a mendicant meditates perceiving before and behind:
translation_text.sn51.20:7.5: as before, so behind; as behind, so before.
translation_text.sn51.20:8.1: And how does a mendicant meditate as below, so above; as above, so below?
translation_text.sn51.20:8.2: It’s when a mendicant examines their own body up from the soles of the feet and down from the tips of the hairs, wrapped in skin and full of many kinds of filth.
translation_text.sn51.20:8.3: ‘In this body there is head hair, body hair, nails, teeth, skin, flesh, sinews, bones, bone marrow, kidneys, heart, liver, diaphragm, spleen, lungs, intestines, mesentery, undigested food, feces, bile, phlegm, pus, blood, sweat, fat, tears, grease, saliva, snot, synovial fluid, urine.’
translation_text.sn51.20:8.4: That’s how a mendicant meditates as below, so above; as above, so below.
translation_text.sn51.20:9.1: And how does a mendicant meditate as by day, so by night; as by night, so by day?
translation_text.sn51.20:9.2: It’s when a mendicant develops the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to enthusiasm, and active effort, with the same features, attributes, and signs by day as by night.
translation_text.sn51.20:9.3: And they develop it with the same features, attributes, and signs by night as by day.
translation_text.sn51.20:9.4: That’s how a mendicant meditates as by day, so by night; as by night, so by day.
translation_text.sn51.20:10.1: And how, with an open and unenveloped heart, does a mendicant develop a mind that’s full of radiance?
translation_text.sn51.20:10.2: It’s when a mendicant has properly grasped the perception of light, and has properly grasped the perception of day.
translation_text.sn51.20:10.3: That’s how, with an open and unenveloped heart, a mendicant develops a mind that’s full of radiance.
translation_text.sn51.20:11.1: And what is energy that’s too lax? …
translation_text.sn51.20:16.1: And what is mental development that’s too lax? …
translation_text.sn51.20:20.1: And what is inquiry that’s too lax?
translation_text.sn51.20:20.2: It’s when inquiry is coupled with laziness.
translation_text.sn51.20:21.1: And what is inquiry that’s too tense?
translation_text.sn51.20:21.2: It’s when inquiry is coupled with restlessness.
translation_text.sn51.20:22.1: And what is inquiry that’s constricted internally?
translation_text.sn51.20:22.2: It’s when inquiry is coupled with dullness and drowsiness.
translation_text.sn51.20:22.3: This is called inquiry constricted internally.
translation_text.sn51.20:23.1: And what is inquiry that’s scattered externally?
translation_text.sn51.20:23.2: It’s when inquiry is frequently scattered and diffused externally on account of the five kinds of sensual stimulation.
translation_text.sn51.20:23.3: This is called inquiry scattered externally. …
translation_text.sn51.20:23.4: That’s how, with an open and unenveloped heart, a mendicant develops a mind that’s full of radiance.
translation_text.sn51.20:23.5: When the four bases of psychic power have been developed and cultivated in this way they’re very fruitful and beneficial.
translation_text.sn51.20:24.1: When the four bases of psychic power have been developed and cultivated in this way, a mendicant wields the many kinds of psychic power: multiplying themselves and becoming one again … controlling the body as far as the realm of divinity.
translation_text.sn51.20:24.2: When the four bases of psychic power have been developed and cultivated in this way, they realize the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life. And they live having realized it with their own insight due to the ending of defilements.”
